data.title: [Cleanup] Navbar Jacquard sizes: convert fixed-px (82/50/42/48px) to clamp() matching existing mobile-variant pattern

data.to:
**[CRITICAL CLEANUP]**

Per FRAC-21 §C4: Navbar uses fixed-pixel Jacquard font sizes that break the 375px mobile baseline.

## What's wrong
- `src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx:163` — desktop wordmark `fontSize: "82px"`
- `Navbar.tsx:200` — `fontSize: "48px"`
- `Navbar.tsx:255` — `fontSize: "50px"`
- `Navbar.tsx:169` — `fontSize: "42px"`

The author **already knows the responsive pattern** — mobile-menu variants use `clamp(42px, 8vw, 82px)` (`:323`) and `clamp(27px, 5vw, 48px)` (`:329`). The fixed-px sizes are inconsistencies, not principled choices.

## Action

Convert every fixed-px Jacquard font-size in Navbar.tsx to a `clamp(min, vw-based, max)` matching the existing mobile-variant pattern. Choose `vw` values that produce ~the same desktop appearance at 1440+ but stay under viewport at 375px.

Specifically:
- `82px` → `clamp(42px, 8vw, 82px)` (already proven by `:323`)
- `50px` → `clamp(28px, 5.5vw, 50px)`
- `48px` → `clamp(27px, 5vw, 48px)` (already proven by `:329`)
- `42px` → `clamp(24px, 4.5vw, 42px)`

## Acceptance criteria

- All Navbar Jacquard font-sizes use `clamp()` not fixed-px
- 375px screenshot: no horizontal overflow or text wrap on Navbar elements
- 1440px screenshot: visually matches current desktop appearance

## Cites
FRAC-21 synthesis §C4

data.body: Review (orchestrator, fresh context): diff shows 4 fontSize literals → clamp(), exactly per plan. Build 3.59s, 0 errors. Tests match master baseline (141 pass, 4 pre-existing fail, none related). Branch frac-29-navbar-clamp ready.
